---
title: "May Flowers"
format: html
author: "Marlin Alejandro"
---

*Here are the may flowers, and there are plenty on campus.*

The days are approaching, the memories fleeting, like the cherry blossoms on campus. My college life seems to be slipping from my fingers and with it the end of my teenage years and the beginning of my 20s.

I often feel as if I missed out on milestones that people my age are expected to reach, but then again, I am not everyone, am I?

In my Ecopoetics class, we are required to have a journal: nature journal. Meant for our thoughts as we contemplate and consider our relationship with nature and where we stand. I have taken a liking to going on walks around campus after class. The best thing about this spring semester is the lack of restraints on my schedule. I have actually not written much in my journal, not from lack of thinking, but rather because I tend to carry my own personal journal.

### Graduation

Graduation came and there was nothing much to say. I wanted to somehow leave a part of me at Wellesley College, so that she would remember me and to prove that I was there. How to leave behind my print?

The graduation passed by quickly-- I was sitting one moment for rehearsal and then I was walking on stage, waving at a sea of people whose faces I could not see. I graduated, did you see?

妹妹,这仍在进行中
